description_long: This triumphal arch is a robust stone structure featuring three archways, with the central arch being the largest and flanked by two smaller arches. The exterior is finished in a dark, weathered stone texture, giving it an aged and monumental appearance. The top of the arch is flat and broad, with a slightly curved profile that adds to its grandeur. The sides are solid and rectangular, with no visible decorative columns or pilasters. There are no visible sculptural reliefs or ornate carvings, emphasizing a more utilitarian and austere design. The structure is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles, commonly found as monumental gateways in urban environments.
description: A large, dark stone triumphal arch with three archways and a flat, broad top, featuring a weathered, monumental appearance and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing three archways, with the central arch larger than the two side arches.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, rectangular profile and the flat top of the arch.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the three archways and the broad, flat top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the solid side and flat roof.
